sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the pattern:
- Before: purple square at [[52, 9], [52, 11], [54, 11], [54, 9]], gray dot at [[51, 12]]
- After: purple square at [[50, 11], [50, 13], [52, 13], [52, 11]], gray dot at [[53, 10]] (new position)
So clicking on a gray dot moves the purple square to that gray dot's position, and the old purple square position becomes a gray dot. The purple square moves one step along the path toward the blue blob.
The path goes from bottom-left to top-right. The purple square is moving up the path. I need to keep clicking the next gray dot along the path.
Current purple square: [[50, 11], [50, 13], [52, 13], [52, 11]] - center around (51, 12)
Next gray dot along path toward blue blob: (49, 14)
Let me continue clicking along the path. The gray dots go: (49,14), (47,16), (45,18), (43,20), (41,22), (39,24), (37,26), (35,28), (33,30), (31,32), (29,34), (27,36), (25,38), (23,40), (21,42), (19,44), (17,46)
Then the blue blob starts at around (17,44)-(19,52). The target seems to be the blue blob or the gray 3x3 square inside it at (14-16, 47-49).
I should batch click all the remaining gray dots along the path to move the purple square to the blue blob.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Pattern confirmed: clicking a gray dot moves purple square to that dot's position
# The old purple square position becomes a gray dot
# Path goes up-right toward blue blob
# Current purple: ~center (51,12), next dot: (49,14)
# Need to click all remaining gray dots to reach blue blob

# Gray dots along path (toward blue blob):
# (49,14), (47,16), (45,18), (43,20), (41,22), (39,24), (37,26), (35,28), (33,30), (31,32), (29,34), (27,36), (25,38), (23,40), (21,42), (19,44), (17,46)

# Batch click all remaining dots
path_dots = [
    (49, 14), (47, 16), (45, 18), (43, 20), (41, 22), (39, 24), (37, 26), (35, 28),
    (33, 30), (31, 32), (29, 34), (27, 36), (25, 38), (23, 40), (21, 42), (19, 44), (17, 46)
]

actions = [{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c} for r, c in path_dots]
action(actions)
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
